spinning._comment: These apply to all 'straight' and 'spun' segments by default. Spin period ~0.63 m, modulation index m=10. Pole wraps and paddle controllers are NOT spun because bending dominates or they are separate devices.

sections.name: Underground duct — campus to building A
sections._comment: Buried fiber at stable temperature. Two segments joined by a fusion splice. Each straight segment uses the fiber-level spin parameters. The splice acts as a natural hinge (birefringence axis scrambling at the fusion point).

sections.name: Aerial span — building A to building B

segments._comment: Fiber wrapped around a utility pole. Short (~2.8 m), tightly bent. NOT spun — bending birefringence dominates. Acts as a natural hinge.

sections._comment: Aerial fiber between poles. Straight runs are spun (same spool of fiber). Pole wraps introduce bending birefringence and act as natural hinges. Temperature is higher and more variable than underground.

segments._comment: ThorLabs-style fiber paddle set for manual polarization compensation. NOT spun — it is a separate device that IS the hinge/compensator.

hinge_model._comment: Random rotators are inserted at section boundaries (underground-aerial, aerial-indoor) and at splice points. Pole wraps are NOT given additional hinges because the bending itself scrambles the axes deterministically.
